title: cuscuta planiflora var. calycina
intro: Cuscuta planiflora var. calycina, also known as Dodder, is a parasitic plant that uses its stems to connect to and draw nutrients from other plants, making it a unique and fascinating species. Traditionally, Cuscuta planiflora var. calycina has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use and efficacy may require further scientific research.
